title: A dataset recorded during development of an affective brain-computer music interface: testing session
description: This dataset contains EEG recordings from 8 healthy adult participants during real-time control of an affective brain-computer music interface (BCMI). Participants listened to 60-second music clips designed to induce specific emotional states defined by valence and arousal dimensions. Each 60-second clip consisted of three 20-second segments: an initial segment targeting emotional state A, a middle segment where the brain-computer interface identified the participant's actual affective state and generated music to transition toward target state B, and a final segment targeting state B. The dataset includes continuous EEG data sampled at 1 kHz alongside self-reported affective state ratings (valence and arousal), enabling investigation of neural correlates of emotion and the efficacy of real-time music-based neurofeedback for emotional state modulation.
methods_description: EEG data were recorded at 1 kHz sampling rate from 8 healthy adult participants during listening to 60-second synthetic music clips. Each clip consisted of three 20-second segments: an initial segment targeting emotional state A, a middle segment where the brain-computer interface identified the participant's actual affective state and generated music to transition toward target state B, and a final segment targeting state B. Participants provided self-reported valence and arousal ratings for each clip. The synthetic music generator used to generate the music clips was presented in Williams et al. (2017).
